nodes.label: GLOBAL PATHOGEN SPREAD
nodes.description: The complete interconnected framework of pathogen emergence, transport, and establishment.

nodes.label: Infection buildup in vegetation
nodes.gap_desc: Cannot detect real-time infection state globally
nodes.why_matters: Prevents identification of true source regions

nodes.label: Spore release under drying + wind
nodes.observable: Timing and magnitude of airborne inoculum release
nodes.gap_desc: No emission functions linking biology + weather
nodes.why_matters: Cannot predict when spread begins

nodes.label: Host phenological transition
nodes.gap_desc: Phenology not tied to pathogen lifecycle
nodes.why_matters: Weak source timing estimation

nodes.label: Meteorological triggering
nodes.gap_desc: No direct coupling of weather → release
nodes.future_dev:
1. Measurements at both source and receptor regions
2. Laboratory based experiments to study specific properties
nodes.why_matters: Cannot parameterize emission

nodes.label: Air parcel movement
nodes.gap_desc: Biology treated as passive particles
nodes.why_matters: Overestimates spread accuracy

nodes.label: Vertical lofting/ long distance transport
nodes.future_dev:
1. Multi-scale & Multi-dimensional Model -Measurement -Integration
2. Model parameterization advancement/development for accurate representation of processes  to decrease model uncertainties and improve future predictions.
nodes.why_matters: Cannot estimate long-range transport

nodes.label: Survival during transport
nodes.gap_desc: No survival functions (UV, humidity, lightning)
nodes.why_matters: Major uncertainty in spread probability

nodes.label: Commodity movement between regions
nodes.observable: Volume and direction of susceptible commodity flow
nodes.gap_desc: Only country-level aggregation
nodes.why_matters: Cannot resolve pathway-level spread

nodes.label: Maritime shipping
nodes.gap_desc: No linkage between cargo and pathogen
nodes.why_matters: Cannot identify high-risk flows

nodes.label: Contamination at origin
nodes.observable: Presence of pathogen in shipped commodities
nodes.gap_desc: No contamination detection prior to shipment
nodes.why_matters: Cannot identify true source

nodes.label: Cold-chain storage
nodes.gap_desc: No persistence modeling during transport
nodes.why_matters: Misestimates spread probability

nodes.label: Global road networks and land transit corridors
nodes.observable: Global road networks and land transit corridors
nodes.current_ability: GRIP4 Global Vector Roads, OpenStreetMap (OSM) - Highly detailed vector geometries (sub-meter accuracy); globally available.
nodes.gap_desc:
Limitations: Does not indicate traffic volume or type of goods transported.
Needed: Freight-specific traffic density maps weighted by agricultural, livestock, and cold-chain transport.
nodes.why_matters: Physical land routes (trucking corridors) connecting infected farms to export ports or domestic markets.

nodes.label: Global maritime shipping corridors
nodes.current_ability: Spire Maritime AIS, UN Global Platform AIS - Empirically tracks real vessel movements globally; high-frequency ping resolution (meters).
nodes.gap_desc:
Limitations: Massive data volume; cannot differentiate cargo types directly from vessel pings.
Needed: Cargo-specific maritime tracking (linking vessel ID to bill of lading / reefer container manifests).
nodes.why_matters: Sea-based physical routes connecting export ports to import ports, modeling intercontinental spread.
nodes.candidate_data: Spire Maritime AIS, UN Global Platform AIS

nodes.label: Global aviation and air freight
nodes.current_ability: OpenSky Network, FlightRadar24 - Live and historical flight paths globally (meter resolution).
nodes.gap_desc:
Limitations: Flight data does not specify cargo contents.
Needed: Linking specific flights to agricultural/live cargo manifests and phytosanitary certificates.
nodes.why_matters: Rapid intercontinental transit vectors for perishable goods, vectors (insects in cargo), and human carriers.

nodes.label: Global logistics infrastructure (Ports/Hubs)
nodes.current_ability: World Port Index (WPI), Global Freight Routing Model (GFRM) - Exact point coordinates for global intermodal hubs.
nodes.gap_desc:
Limitations: Lacks data on specific quarantine or phytosanitary inspection capacities at each port.
Needed: Throughput metrics for specific susceptible commodities and localized inspection failure rates.
nodes.why_matters: Nodes connecting land routes to sea/air routes. Essential for multimodal routing algorithms.
nodes.candidate_data: World Port Index (WPI), Global Freight Routing Model (GFRM)

nodes.label: Global shipping and ocean transit density
nodes.observable: Global shipping and ocean transit density
nodes.current_ability: Global Shipping Traffic Density Rasters (2km resolution) - Global coverage; continuous density raster format makes it highly compatible with physical routing algorithms (like Dijkstra or MCP).
nodes.gap_desc:
Limitations: Raster data does not differentiate vessel types (e.g., agricultural vs industrial) and assumes density correlates with probability of transit.
Needed: Commodity-specific routing density rasters (e.g., filtering only reefer/agricultural cargo ships).
nodes.why_matters: Provides continuous cost-surface maps (density grids) for calculating Least-Cost Paths (LCP) across oceans between exit and entry ports.
nodes.candidate_data: Global Shipping Traffic Density Rasters (2km resolution)

nodes.label: Agricultural labor movement
nodes.observable: Movement of workers across agricultural regions
nodes.gap_desc: Cannot isolate agricultural workforce mobility
nodes.why_matters: Misses key spread pathway

nodes.label: Equipment sharing
nodes.gap_desc: No tracking of equipment movement
nodes.why_matters: Misses local spread dynamics

nodes.label: High-resolution ambient human population distribution
nodes.current_ability: LandScan Global Population Database (Oak Ridge National Laboratory - ORNL) - Global coverage; exceptional high spatial resolution (~1 km / 30 arc-seconds); updated annually. Captures daytime/workday population distributions better than nighttime residential census data.
nodes.gap_desc:
Limitations: It is a static density map (average 24-hour period) rather than a dynamic mobility tracking dataset. It shows where people are, but not where they are traveling to (which is needed for mapping long-distance physical spread vectors).
Needed: Real-time, dynamic flow data coupling ambient population density with high-frequency rural-to-urban transit routes.
nodes.why_matters: Provides the ambient (24-hour average) population density, which helps estimate the sheer volume of human interaction, agricultural workforce density, and urban-rural interfaces where fomite transmission (via contaminated hands, clothing, or local vehicles) is most probable.
nodes.candidate_data: LandScan Global Population Database (Oak Ridge National Laboratory - ORNL)

nodes.label: Initial outbreak locations / validated occurrences
nodes.current_ability: CABI PlantwisePlus, EPPO Global Database, WAHIS (Animals), ProMED - High reliability; globally recognized standards for tracking.
nodes.gap_desc:
Limitations: Spatial resolution often limited to country or administrative region; severe reporting delays in developing nations.
Needed: Real-time, farm-level geolocated detection data and genomic sequencing to trace exact origin strains.
nodes.why_matters: Confirmed ground-truth starting points and historical emergence timelines for the physical spread of plant/animal pathogens.
nodes.candidate_data: CABI PlantwisePlus, EPPO Global Database, WAHIS (Animals), ProMED

nodes.label: Migration
nodes.gap_desc: nan
nodes.why_matters: nan

nodes.label: Arrival of pathogen
nodes.gap_desc: No biological deposition measurements
nodes.why_matters: Cannot link transport to impact

nodes.label: Infection after arrival
nodes.gap_desc: No dose-response relationships
nodes.why_matters: Cannot predict outbreak likelihood

nodes.label: Host-pathogen interaction
nodes.observable: Susceptibility of host at time of arrival
nodes.gap_desc: No real-time host vulnerability mapping
nodes.why_matters: Weak prediction accuracy

nodes.label: Host crop presence
nodes.observable: Presence of a target host crop in the USA
nodes.gap_desc: Data is released after the growing season (following year)
nodes.why_matters: Mapping areas with a particular target crop is key to defining the risk of pathogen establishment after its arrival

nodes.label: Host susceptibility
nodes.gap_desc: No available data
nodes.why_matters: For some diseases, host susceptibility depends on the genotype/variety

nodes.label: Alternative host presence
nodes.observable: Presence of alternative host for a particular pathogen
nodes.gap_desc: No available data
nodes.why_matters: Alternative hosts, wich include other crops, weeds, and wild pants, can support the establishment of a pathogen even without the presence of the target host crop

nodes.label: Host susceptibility
nodes.gap_desc: Data is available at the county level and does not provide sufficient detail for defining crop susceptibility
nodes.why_matters: Phenological stage is key to define host susceptibility

nodes.label: Host susceptibility
nodes.gap_desc: Converting vegetation indices into phenology metrics requires additional work and presents limitations in terms of accuracy
nodes.why_matters: Phenological stage is key to define host susceptibility
nodes.candidate_data: EVI/NDVI obtained from satellites such as Sentinel-2 and HLS

nodes.label: Host susceptibility
nodes.gap_desc: No available data
nodes.why_matters: Management practices impact the susceptibility of a host

nodes.label: Host susceptibility
nodes.gap_desc: Water stress can be partially monitored using remote sensing, but other types of stress (previous disease/pest occurrence) can not be easily monitored
nodes.why_matters: Previous stress makes the crop more susceptible to new diseases

nodes.label: Enviromental conditions enabling infection
nodes.gap_desc: Local variations may not be fully captured by the current datasets
nodes.why_matters: Meteorological conditions are important for assessing the risk of infection for a particular pathogen

nodes.label: Field observation of unusual plant disease
nodes.observable: Early indication of abnormal plant health or pathogen presence
nodes.gap_desc: No structured capture of early field observations
nodes.why_matters: Delays detection of emerging threats

nodes.label: Scientific or diagnostic identification
nodes.observable: Detection of novel or known pathogens in labs
nodes.gap_desc: Lab results are delayed, fragmented, and not integrated
nodes.why_matters: Misses early confirmation of outbreaks

nodes.label: Informal communication between actors
nodes.observable: Spread of information through networks (farmers, agronomists, researchers)
nodes.gap_desc: No tracking of informal information flow
nodes.why_matters: Early signals remain localized and unutilized

nodes.label: Formal reporting systems
nodes.observable: Reporting of outbreaks through official channels
nodes.gap_desc: Reporting delays and underreporting
nodes.why_matters: Slows coordinated response

nodes.label: Aggregation of signals into actionable awareness
nodes.observable: Recognition of a pattern indicating an outbreak
nodes.gap_desc: No framework for integrating weak signals across sources
nodes.why_matters: Prevents early warning synthesis

nodes.label: Anomaly emergence across signals
nodes.observable: Deviations from baseline biological or reporting patterns
nodes.gap_desc: No baseline definition of “normal” biological signal
nodes.why_matters: Cannot detect unknown threats

nodes.label: Downstream movement of water-borne pathogens or contaminated agricultural runoff.
nodes.observable: Physical routing and hydrological connectivity of drainage basins.
nodes.current_ability: High-resolution global river networks and catchment boundary definitions exist.
nodes.gap_desc: Limitations: Networks are static topological maps; they don't inherently provide real-time velocity or pathogen decay rates during transit. Needed: Real-time hydrological routing overlaid on static networks.
nodes.why_matters: Essential for tracing an upstream source of infection (e.g., infected farm runoff) to downstream susceptible agricultural regions.
nodes.candidate_data: Global HydroSHEDS (Hydrological data and maps based on SHuttle Elevation Derivatives)

nodes.label: Proliferation of pathogens, algal blooms, or host vectors in major waterways and coastal zones.
nodes.observable: Water color, turbidity, and hyperspectral biological signatures indicating outbreaks or high-risk conditions.
nodes.current_ability: Space-borne hyperspectral sensors are highly capable of observing biological signatures in massive rivers, estuaries, and coastal zones.
nodes.gap_desc: Limitations: Spatial resolution is too coarse for smaller agricultural tributaries/canals; optical sensors are blocked by clouds. Needed: High-resolution hyperspectral monitoring for narrow inland waterways.
nodes.why_matters: Serves as a direct proxy or early warning sign of aquatic biological instability, contamination events, or vector breeding grounds.
nodes.candidate_data: NASA PACE satellite (Plankton, Aerosol, Cloud, ocean Ecosystem)

nodes.label: Extreme low flow conditions leading to high concentrations of pathogens in stagnant water.
nodes.observable: River surface water elevation (stage) and discharge volume over time.
nodes.current_ability: Spaceborne altimetry can accurately measure water surface elevation for medium-to-large rivers globally.
nodes.gap_desc: Limitations: Satellite revisit times can miss rapid drawdowns; altimeters struggle to measure narrow or highly braided streams. Needed: Continuous real-time integration of altimetry with terrestrial river gauges.
nodes.why_matters: Low flow conditions create high-risk stagnant pools for disease vectors (e.g., mosquitoes) and concentrate water-borne pathogens right when farmers are desperate to pump that water for irrigation.
nodes.candidate_data: Spaceborne Altimeters (e.g., NASA/CNES SWOT - Surface Water and Ocean Topography, Sentinel-6)

nodes.label: Global host density (Cropland / Vegetation)
nodes.current_ability: ESA WorldCover, Copernicus Global Land Service (CGLS) - Exceptional spatial resolution (10m to 100m); globally continuous and regularly updated.
nodes.gap_desc:
Limitations: Identifies generic cropland but struggles to differentiate specific crop species on a global scale.
Needed: Dynamic, high-resolution global mapping of specific crop species and greenhouse infrastructure.
nodes.why_matters: Identifies Global high-density agricultural zones where plant pathogens originate or establish.
nodes.candidate_data: ESA WorldCover, Copernicus Global Land Service (CGLS)

nodes.label: Global host density (Cropland / Vegetation)
nodes.current_ability: Google Dynamic World - Exceptional spatial resolution (10m); globally continuous and regularly updated.
nodes.gap_desc:
Limitations: Identifies generic cropland but struggles to differentiate specific crop species on a global scale.
Needed: Dynamic, high-resolution global mapping of specific crop species and greenhouse infrastructure.
nodes.why_matters: Identifies Global high-density agricultural zones where plant pathogens originate or establish.

nodes.label: Global host density (Livestock / Animals)
nodes.current_ability: FAO Gridded Livestock of the World (GLW 4) - Global coverage; species-specific (cattle, poultry, swine). Resolution: ~10 km (0.083 degrees).
nodes.gap_desc:
Limitations: Static yearly datasets; coarse 10km resolution.
Needed: High-resolution, dynamic tracking of concentrated animal feeding operations (CAFOs) and pastoral movement.
nodes.why_matters: Identifies high-density livestock zones for zoonotic or animal-borne pathogens.
nodes.candidate_data: FAO Gridded Livestock of the World (GLW 4)

nodes.label: Soil and terrain baselines (Soil-borne pathogens)
nodes.gap_desc:
Limitations: Static baseline; does not track real-time soil microbiomes.
Needed: Farm-level localized soil pathogen baselines and real-time soil health monitoring.
nodes.why_matters: Determines if soil conditions (pH, texture, moisture) at destination are conducive to establishment.

nodes.label: Disruption & Rerouting
nodes.observable: Socio-political instability, conflicts, or port strikes severing standard physical transport routes.
nodes.current_ability: AI platforms can predict and detect political violence, protests, or border closures in real-time.
nodes.gap_desc:
Limitations: Risk platforms track human/political events, not biological events.
Needed: Direct integration of risk API triggers with physical routing algorithms.
nodes.why_matters: Identifies when standard Lines of Communication break down, forcing trade through unregulated back-channels.

nodes.label: Amplification & Verification
nodes.observable: Media sentiment panics, localized reporting of crop failures, or food security riots preceding official biological declarations.
nodes.current_ability: Massive global language scraping can detect localized sentiment shifts and news density in near real-time.
nodes.gap_desc:
Limitations: News data is a lagging indicator for initial biological emergence.
Needed: Algorithms to filter out market noise and isolate specific crop-failure discussions as an early warning.
nodes.why_matters: Helps triangulate the actual spread of a pathogen by tracking the human socio-economic reaction rather than the physical virus itself.
